2603.14323.title: How Do Medical MLLMs Fail? A Study on Visual Grounding in Medical Images
2603.14323.paper_summary: Generalist multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have achieved impressive performance across a wide range of vision-language tasks. However, their performance on medical tasks, particularly in zero-shot settings where generalization is critical, remains suboptimal. A key research gap is the limited understanding of why medical MLLMs underperform in medical image interpretation. In this work, we present a pioneering systematic investigation into the visual grounding capabilities of state-of-the-art medical MLLMs. To disentangle visual grounding from semantic grounding, we design VGMED, a novel evaluation dataset developed with expert clinical guidance, explicitly assessing the visual grounding capability of medical MLLMs. We introduce new quantitative metrics and conduct detailed qualitative analyses. Our study across eight state-of-the-art (SOTA) medical MLLMs validates that they often fail to ground their predictions in clinically relevant image regions. We note that this finding is specific to medical image analysis; in contrast, prior work has shown that MLLMs are capable of grounding their predictions in the correct image regions when applied to natural scene images. Motivated by these findings, we propose VGRefine, a simple yet effective inference-time method that refines attention distribution to improve visual grounding in medical settings. Our approach achieves SOTA performance across 6 diverse Med-VQA benchmarks (over 110K VQA samples from 8 imaging modalities) without requiring additional training or external expert models. Overall, our work, for the first time, systematically validates inadequate visual grounding as one of the key contributing factors for medical MLLMs' under-performance. Additional experiments are included in the Supp.
2603.14323.paper_summary_zh: 通用多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）在各種視覺-語言任務中取得了令人印象深刻的表現。<lb/>然而，它們在醫療任務上的表現，特別是在零樣本環境中，當泛化至關重要時，仍然不理想。<lb/>一個關鍵的研究空白是對於為什麼醫療 MLLMs 在醫療影像解釋中表現不佳的理解有限。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了一項開創性的系統性調查，研究最先進的醫療 MLLMs 的視覺基礎能力。<lb/>為了將視覺基礎與語義基礎區分開來，我們設計了 VGMED，一個在專家臨床指導下開發的新評估數據集，明確評估醫療 MLLMs 的視覺基礎能力。<lb/>我們引入了新的定量指標並進行了詳細的定性分析。<lb/>我們對八個最先進的（SOTA）醫療 MLLMs 的研究驗證了它們經常無法將預測與臨床相關的影像區域相結合。<lb/>我們注意到，這一發現特定於醫療影像分析；相反，先前的研究表明，當應用於自然場景影像時，MLLMs 能夠將其預測與正確的影像區域相結合。<lb/>受到這些發現的激勵，我們提出了 VGRefine，一種簡單但有效的推理時方法，通過改進注意力分佈來提高醫療環境中的視覺基礎。<lb/>我們的方法在六個不同的 Med-VQA 基準上達到了 SOTA 表現（來自八種影像模態的超過 110K VQA 樣本），而無需額外的訓練或外部專家模型。<lb/>總的來說，我們的工作首次系統性地驗證了不足的視覺基礎是醫療 MLLMs 表現不佳的關鍵因素之一。<lb/>附加實驗包含在補充材料中。
2603.14323.author: Guimeng Liu et.al.
2603.14323.authors: Guimeng Liu, Tianze Yu, Somayeh Ebrahimkhani, Lin Zhi Zheng Shawn, Kok Pin Ng, Ngai-Man Cheung
